title: Experterna om läget i Evolution: "En väldigt speciell situation"
summary_md:
# Experterna om läget i Evolution: "En väldigt speciell situation"

**Feed**: dagen-med-di  
**Published**: 2026-06-26  
**Market Sentiment**: Mixed

⏱️ **Time Horizon Key**: ST = <3 months | MT = 3-12 months | LT = >12 months

---

## Episode Thesis

The episode is a panel discussion with guests Carl Holve and Mikael Olsson about the growing trend of share buyback programs on the Stockholm exchange versus short-selling pressure. The main focus is Evolution's unusually large and aggressive buyback program in the context of Dart's rise to a 30% ownership stake, plus Norion as last year's most interesting buyback case. Broader themes include a structural shift among US tech giants away from buybacks toward AI capex and equity issuance.

---

## Universe Stocks

### 🟢 NORION — Norion | **BUY** (Moderate)

**Sentiment**: Positive | **Time Horizon**: ST  
**Confidence**: 60%

Ongoing aggressive buyback program with remaining capacity; stock rebounded 50→60 kr; Erik Selin buying more.

#### Reasoning

Host considers Norion last year's best buyback case. The active buyback creates constant demand supporting the price, and management/Erik Selin continue buying. Host expects the stock to keep performing strongly as long as buybacks continue, though notes valuation has nearly doubled from the 35 kr starting point so management should not be indifferent to price.

#### Sell Triggers

- if the buyback program ends without renewal
- if no new buyback program is launched
- if valuation is considered too stretched after the run-up

> *"Jag tror att kursen kommer att fortsätta gå starkt– –givet att man återköper så pass mycket tills det tar slut."*

---

### 🟠 EVO — Evolution | **WATCH** (Weak)

**Sentiment**: Mixed | **Time Horizon**: MT  
**Confidence**: 45%

Very special situation: massive buyback (~30% of float), Dart at 30% ownership near bid limit, strong cash flows but questioned by market.

#### Reasoning

Evolution has shifted from market favourite to more questioned. The aggressive buyback (~30% of float, >100m SEK/day) provides constant demand supporting the price and signals confidence given strong cash flows. Dart sits at 30%; cancellation of bought-back shares passively pushes past the bid threshold without triggering a mandatory bid. Hosts see no clear problem with the aggressive program but flag uncertainty around an undisclosed swap arrangement.

#### Sell Triggers

- if cash flow generation weakens
- if the swap-agreement details prove unfavourable
- if dividend-strategy funds continue forced selling

> *"Det är en väldigt speciell situation i Evolution där den här Dart har kommit in som huvudägare."*

---
